1.title: Lee Trevino, six-time major champion, is born
1.text: Lee Trevino was born December 1, 1939, in Dallas, Texas. The self-taught "Merry Mex" won six majors — two U.S. Opens, two Open Championships and two PGA Championships — and ranks among golf's greatest shotmakers.

1.title: Wayne Riley wins the Australian Open at Royal Melbourne
1.text: Australia's Wayne Riley closed with three straight birdies, including a 40-foot putt on the last, to win the 1991 Australian Open by one stroke over Robert Allenby at Royal Melbourne.

1.title: Montgomerie beats Els in Sun City Million Dollar playoff
1.text: Colin Montgomerie sank a birdie putt on the third playoff hole to beat Ernie Els and win the 1996 Million Dollar Challenge at the Gary Player Country Club, Sun City.

1.title: Mark O'Meara wins the Skins Game
1.text: On December 1, 2002, Mark O'Meara won the Skins Game at Landmark Golf Club in Indio, California, taking eight skins worth $405,000 ahead of Tiger Woods, Phil Mickelson and Fred Couples.

1.title: Lonard and Moseley share Australian PGA in fading light
1.text: On December 1, 2002, Peter Lonard and Jarrod Moseley tied at 17-under at the Australian PGA Championship; after one playoff hole, fading light forced them to share the title.

1.title: Rory McIlroy edges Adam Scott to win Australian Open
1.text: On December 1, 2013, Rory McIlroy closed with a 6-under 66 at Royal Sydney, overhauling a four-shot deficit to beat Adam Scott by one stroke and capture the Emirates Australian Open.

1.title: Larrazabal wins Alfred Dunhill Championship at Leopard Creek
1.text: Battling injury, Spain's Pablo Larrazabal held on to win the 2019 Alfred Dunhill Championship by one stroke over Joel Sjoholm at Leopard Creek, finishing at 8-under 280.

1.title: Ryggs Johnston wins the Australian Open
1.text: On December 1, 2024, 24-year-old American Ryggs Johnston, ranked 954th in the world, closed with a 68 at Kingston Heath to win the Australian Open by three over Curtis Luck.

2.title: Bill Mehlhorn, 19-time PGA Tour winner, is born
2.text: Bill Mehlhorn, a 19-time PGA Tour winner of the 1920s, a member of the first U.S. Ryder Cup team in 1927 and the 1924 Western Open champion, was born December 2, 1898.

2.title: Paraguay's Marco Ruiz is born
2.text: Marco Ruiz, a Paraguayan professional who won the 2007 Argentine Open and played The Open Championship twice, was born December 2, 1974, in Asuncion.

2.title: Floyds defend Father/Son Challenge title
2.text: Raymond Floyd and son Robert won the 2001 Office Depot Father/Son Challenge on December 2, edging Hale and Steve Irwin by one shot in the Bahamas for Ray Floyd's fifth title in the event.

2.title: Immelman edges Rose to win Nedbank Challenge
2.text: Trevor Immelman beat Justin Rose by one stroke at the Gary Player Country Club on December 2, 2007, to win the Nedbank Golf Challenge at Sun City, months before his 2008 Masters triumph.

2.title: Martin Kaymer wins Nedbank Golf Challenge at Sun City
2.text: Germany's Martin Kaymer closed with a 69 in a rainy finale to win the Nedbank Golf Challenge by two strokes over Charl Schwartzel at Sun City, his first title since 2011.

2.title: Cameron Smith defends Australian PGA Championship title
2.text: Cameron Smith closed with a 2-under 70 at RACV Royal Pines to win the Australian PGA Championship by two strokes over Marc Leishman, successfully defending his 2017 title.

2.title: Jon Rahm wins Hero World Challenge by four
2.text: Jon Rahm closed with a 7-under 65 for a four-shot win over Tony Finau at the Hero World Challenge in the Bahamas on December 2, 2018, becoming the first Spaniard to win Tiger Woods's event.

3.title: USA wins World Cup of Golf in Hawaii
3.text: The United States team of John Mahaffey and Andy North won the 1978 World Cup of Golf by 10 strokes at Princeville, Kauai, Hawaii, with the event concluding on December 3.

3.title: Peter Senior wins the 1989 Australian Open
3.text: Peter Senior won the 1989 Australian Open at Kingston Heath, with the championship contested November 30 through December 3.

3.title: Davis Love III wins Tiger Woods' World Challenge
3.text: Davis Love III closed with a 64 for a 22-under 266 to win the 2000 Williams World Challenge (now the Hero World Challenge) at Sherwood Country Club, holding off host Tiger Woods by two shots.

3.title: Ernie Els defends Nedbank Golf Challenge at Sun City
3.text: Ernie Els defended his Nedbank Golf Challenge title at Sun City, beating Lee Westwood in a playoff to claim the $2 million first prize, with the final round on December 3, 2000.

3.title: Jim Furyk wins the 2006 Nedbank Golf Challenge
3.text: Jim Furyk won the 2006 Nedbank Golf Challenge at Sun City on Sunday, December 3, defeating Henrik Stenson by two strokes.

3.title: Rickie Fowler fires record 61 to win Hero World Challenge
3.text: Rickie Fowler opened the final round with seven straight birdies and shot an 11-under 61 — a tournament record — to win the Hero World Challenge by four shots at Albany in the Bahamas.

3.title: Cameron Smith wins the 2017 Australian PGA Championship
3.text: Cameron Smith won the 2017 Australian PGA Championship at RACV Royal Pines on the Gold Coast, beating Jordan Zunic in a playoff on the December 3 final day.

3.title: Scottie Scheffler defends Hero World Challenge title
3.text: World No. 1 Scottie Scheffler played a bogey-free final-round 68 to finish 20-under and beat Sepp Straka by three shots, defending his Hero World Challenge title at Albany in the Bahamas.

4.title: Two-time PGA Tour winner Matt Every is born
4.text: Matt Every was born December 4, 1983, in Daytona Beach, Florida. He won back-to-back Arnold Palmer Invitational titles in 2014 and 2015 and earned the 2006 Ben Hogan Award as the top U.S. college golfer.

4.title: PGA Tour golfer Harry Higgs is born
4.text: Harry Higgs was born December 4, 1991. He memorably finished tied for fourth at the 2021 PGA Championship and reached a career-high world ranking of 91st.

4.title: Sahith Theegala, PGA Tour winner, is born
4.text: Sahith Theegala was born December 4, 1997, in Orange, California. He swept the 2020 Haskins, Ben Hogan and Nicklaus awards as the top college player and won the 2023 Fortinet Championship.

4.title: Jim Furyk wins 2005 Nedbank Golf Challenge in playoff
4.text: Jim Furyk chipped in for birdie on the second extra hole to win a four-man playoff at Sun City on Sunday, December 4 — the largest playoff in the event's history.

4.title: Lee Westwood wins the 2011 Nedbank Golf Challenge
4.text: Lee Westwood beat Robert Karlsson by two strokes at the Gary Player Country Club, Sun City, with the final round played Sunday, December 4, 2011.

4.title: Tiger Woods ends drought at Chevron World Challenge
4.text: Tiger Woods birdied his final two holes to win the Chevron World Challenge at Sherwood Country Club, beating Zach Johnson by one stroke at 10-under — his first win in over two years.

4.title: Matsuyama holds on to win Hero World Challenge
4.text: Hideki Matsuyama closed with a 73 to win the Hero World Challenge at Albany, finishing 18-under for a two-shot win over Henrik Stenson — his fourth victory in five starts.

4.title: Viktor Hovland wins the 2022 Hero World Challenge
4.text: Viktor Hovland held on at Albany Golf Club in the Bahamas to win the Hero World Challenge for the second straight year, closing out the title in the final round on December 4, 2022.

5.title: Lanny Wadkins, 1977 PGA Champion, is born
5.text: Lanny Wadkins was born December 5, 1949. He won 21 PGA Tour titles including the 1977 PGA Championship — the first major decided by sudden-death playoff — and played on eight U.S. Ryder Cup teams.

5.title: Ernie Els wins Sun City with record 263
5.text: On December 5, 1999, Ernie Els won the Nedbank Million Dollar Challenge at Sun City by five strokes over Colin Montgomerie, posting a tournament-record 25-under 263.

5.title: Graeme McDowell beats Tiger Woods in playoff
5.text: On December 5, 2010, Graeme McDowell holed a clutch birdie at the last to force a playoff, then beat host Tiger Woods at the first extra hole to win the Chevron World Challenge at Sherwood, both at 16-under.

5.title: Geoff Ogilvy wins his first Australian Open
5.text: On December 5, 2010, Geoff Ogilvy closed with a 69 to win the Australian Open at The Lakes by four strokes at 19-under 269, claiming his national open for the first time.

5.title: Viktor Hovland wins the Hero World Challenge
5.text: On December 5, 2021, Viktor Hovland rallied from six back of Collin Morikawa with a 6-under 66 to win the Hero World Challenge at Albany, Bahamas, by one over Scottie Scheffler.

6.title: Per-Ulrik Johansson, Ryder Cup star, is born
6.text: Swedish golfer Per-Ulrik Johansson was born December 6, 1966. He won six European Tour titles and was the first Swede to play on two winning Ryder Cup teams, in 1995 and 1997.

6.title: Greg Chalmers wins the Holden Australian Open
6.text: On December 6, 1998, 25-year-old Greg Chalmers closed with a 70 for even-par 288 to win the Australian Open at Royal Adelaide by one stroke over Peter Senior and Stuart Appleby.

6.title: Nick Price beats Tiger Woods in Sun City playoff
6.text: On December 6, 1998, Nick Price beat Tiger Woods with a birdie at the fifth extra hole to win the Nedbank Million Dollar Challenge at Sun City, both having tied at 15-under 273.

6.title: Adam Scott wins his first Australian Open
6.text: On December 6, 2009, Adam Scott shot an even-par 72 to win the Australian Open at NSW Golf Club by five strokes over Stuart Appleby at 15-under 273, his first win in his home country.

6.title: Jim Furyk wins the Chevron World Challenge
6.text: On December 6, 2009, Jim Furyk closed with a 5-under 67 for a one-shot win over Graeme McDowell at Sherwood — his first victory since the 2007 Canadian Open.

6.title: Bubba Watson wins the Hero World Challenge
6.text: On December 6, 2015, Bubba Watson shot a 6-under 66 to win the Hero World Challenge at Albany, Bahamas, by three strokes over Patrick Reed at 25-under 263.

7.title: Mason Phelps, 1904 Olympic golf gold medalist, is born
7.text: American amateur Mason Phelps, who won team gold at the 1904 Olympics and took the Western Amateur in 1908 and 1910, was born December 7, 1885.

7.title: Luke Donald, former world No. 1, is born
7.text: English golfer Luke Donald was born December 7, 1977, in Hemel Hempstead. He reached world No. 1 and in 2011 became the first player to top both the PGA Tour and European Tour money lists in one season.

7.title: Billy Horschel, 2014 FedEx Cup champion, is born
7.text: American golfer Billy Horschel was born December 7, 1986. In 2014 he won the BMW Championship and Tour Championship in back-to-back weeks to claim the FedEx Cup.

7.title: Nick Price wins Sun City Million Dollar Challenge
7.text: Nick Price closed out the 1997 Million Dollar Challenge at Sun City's Gary Player Country Club, beating Ernie Els and Davis Love III by three shots for his second title in the lucrative limited-field event.

7.title: Jordan Spieth routs field at Hero World Challenge
7.text: Jordan Spieth fired a closing 66 to win the Hero World Challenge at Isleworth by 10 shots over Henrik Stenson, setting an event scoring record at 26-under for his first win of the year.

7.title: Henrik Stenson wins Hero World Challenge in Bahamas
7.text: Henrik Stenson shot a final-round 66 at Albany to win the Hero World Challenge by one stroke over defending champion Jon Rahm, holding off Tiger Woods and Justin Thomas in the Bahamas.

7.title: Matsuyama wins Hero World Challenge in playoff
7.text: Hideki Matsuyama beat Alex Noren in a sudden-death playoff at Albany after both finished 22-under 266, closing with a final-round 64 to claim the $1 million first prize.

8.title: Steve Elkington, 1995 PGA Champion, is born
8.text: Australian golfer Steve Elkington was born December 8, 1962. He won the 1995 PGA Championship at Riviera, beating Colin Montgomerie in a playoff, and claimed the Vardon Trophy that year.

8.title: Brandt Snedeker, 2012 FedEx Cup champion, is born
8.text: American golfer Brandt Snedeker was born December 8, 1980, in Nashville. He won the 2012 FedEx Cup at the Tour Championship and later shot a 59 at the 2018 Wyndham Championship.

8.title: Harrington holds off Woods at Target World Challenge
8.text: Padraig Harrington closed with a 71 at Sherwood Country Club to win Tiger Woods' Target World Challenge by two strokes over the host, claiming the $1 million top prize on December 8, 2002.

8.title: Zach Johnson stuns Tiger Woods in playoff at Sherwood
8.text: Zach Johnson rallied from four back, holed out for par on the 72nd hole, then beat world No. 1 Tiger Woods on the first playoff hole to win the Northwestern Mutual World Challenge at Sherwood.

8.title: Scheffler defends Hero World Challenge title
8.text: Scottie Scheffler closed with a bogey-free 9-under 63 to win by six strokes at Albany, capping a season of nine wins that included the Masters and Olympic gold.

8.title: Veerman wins Nedbank Golf Challenge at Sun City
8.text: Johannes Veerman won the Nedbank Golf Challenge at the Gary Player Country Club by one shot at 5-under 283 for his second DP World Tour title.

9.title: Orville Moody, unlikely 1969 U.S. Open champion, is born
9.text: Orville 'Sarge' Moody was born in Chickasha, Oklahoma. A career Army man, he won the 1969 U.S. Open as his only PGA Tour title, qualifying through both local and sectional stages.

9.title: Tom Kite, 1992 U.S. Open champion, is born
9.text: Tom Kite was born in Austin, Texas. A steady, hard-working professional, he became the PGA Tour's all-time leading money winner and captured his lone major at the windswept 1992 U.S. Open at Pebble Beach.

9.title: Peter Thomson wins Senior PGA Championship
9.text: Five-time Open champion Peter Thomson won the 46th Senior PGA Championship at PGA National at two-under 286, three shots clear of Don January. It was part of a dominant nine-win senior season.

9.title: Wyndham Clark, two-time U.S. Open champion, is born
9.text: Wyndham Clark was born in Denver, Colorado. He broke through with his first major at the 2023 U.S. Open at Los Angeles Country Club and reached the world's top ten.

9.title: International team builds lead at Presidents Cup
9.text: At Royal Melbourne, the International side built a commanding lead over the United States en route to its first Presidents Cup title, completed days later by a 20.5 to 11.5 margin.

10.title: Don Bies, PGA Tour and senior winner, is born
10.text: Don Bies was born in Cottonwood, Idaho. He earned his lone PGA Tour title at the 1975 Sammy Davis Jr.-Greater Hartford Open and went on to win seven times on the Senior PGA Tour.

10.title: Bill Dunk, Australian golf great, is born
10.text: Bill Dunk was born in New South Wales, Australia. He won five Australian PGA Championships and more than 100 tournaments at home, breaking over 80 course records across his career.

10.title: Simon Owen, New Zealand golfer, is born
10.text: Simon Owen was born in New Zealand. He is best remembered for dueling Jack Nicklaus down the stretch at the 1978 Open Championship at St Andrews before finishing runner-up.

10.title: Ben Hogan plays first full round after near-fatal crash
10.text: On December 10, 1949, Ben Hogan played his first full round of golf at Colonial Country Club, roughly ten months after a head-on bus collision nearly killed him. He would win the 1950 U.S. Open the following year.

10.title: Palmer and Nicklaus win PGA National Team Championship
10.text: Arnold Palmer and Jack Nicklaus combined for rounds of 63-66-63-64 to win the PGA National Team Championship at 256, a rare pairing of the game's two biggest stars in the same team.

11.title: Brad Bryant, late-blooming PGA Tour winner, is born
11.text: Brad Bryant was born in Texas. Nicknamed 'Dr. Dirt,' he waited 20 years and 475 starts before winning the 1995 Walt Disney World/Oldsmobile Classic, one of the longest waits for a first Tour title.

11.title: European golfer Romain Wattel is born
11.text: French golfer Romain Wattel was born December 11, 1990. A standout amateur, he won the 2015 Madeira Islands Open on the European Tour and represented France internationally.

11.title: Matt McCarty, PGA Tour winner, is born
11.text: American golfer Matt McCarty was born December 11, 1997. He won his first PGA Tour title at the 2024 Black Desert Championship just weeks after graduating from the Korn Ferry Tour.

11.title: Presidents Cup opens at Royal Melbourne
11.text: The 1998 Presidents Cup got underway at Royal Melbourne, where the International team would rout the United States 20.5 to 11.5 for its first victory in the event.

11.title: Tiger Woods announces leave of absence from golf
11.text: Tiger Woods announced an indefinite leave from professional golf, saying he needed to focus on being a better husband and father, weeks after his November car crash and personal scandal.

12.title: George Grant's wooden golf tee patented
12.text: Boston dentist George Franklin Grant received a U.S. patent for a wooden golf tee on December 12, 1899, an early forerunner of the simple peg that golfers worldwide still use to elevate the ball.

12.title: LPGA winner Shirley Englehorn is born
12.text: Shirley Englehorn was born in Caldwell, Idaho. She won 11 LPGA Tour events including the 1970 LPGA Championship before retiring in 1979.

12.title: Nate Lashley, PGA Tour winner, is born
12.text: Nate Lashley was born in Scottsbluff, Nebraska. He earned an emotional first PGA Tour victory at the 2019 Rocket Mortgage Classic in Detroit, winning by six shots.

12.title: Tiger Woods wins Target World Challenge
12.text: Tiger Woods closed with a 66 to win his own Target World Challenge at Sherwood Country Club at 16-under 268, two strokes ahead of Padraig Harrington.

12.title: International team builds command at Presidents Cup
12.text: On the Saturday at Royal Melbourne, the International side extended its lead over the United States in fourball and foursomes play, setting up its dominant 20.5 to 11.5 Presidents Cup victory.

13.title: Two-time PGA Tour winner Mike Sullivan is born
13.text: American golfer Mike Sullivan was born December 13, 1955. He won three times on the PGA Tour, including the 1980 Southern Open and the 1989 Independent Insurance Agent Open.

13.title: Rickie Fowler, popular PGA Tour star, is born
13.text: Rickie Fowler was born in Murrieta, California. Known for his flair and his 2015 Players Championship win, he finished in the top five of all four majors in 2014.

13.title: European Tour winner Felipe Aguilar is born
13.text: Chilean golfer Felipe Aguilar was born this day. He won the 2008 Indonesia Open and the 2014 Trophee Hassan II on the European Tour during a long international career.

13.title: International team wins Presidents Cup at Royal Melbourne
13.text: The International team broke through for its first Presidents Cup title, routing the United States 20.5 to 11.5 at Royal Melbourne, still the Internationals' only victory in the event.

13.title: Patrick Cantlay wins Las Vegas event playoff era
13.text: Patrick Cantlay, born and bred in California, capped a strong stretch of 2020 golf; his consistency that year foreshadowed his 2021 FedEx Cup title and Player of the Year honors.

14.title: Billy Burke, 1931 U.S. Open champion, is born
14.text: Billy Burke was born in Naugatuck, Connecticut. He won the 1931 U.S. Open in a marathon 72-hole double playoff over George Von Elm, becoming the first to win the title with steel-shafted clubs.

14.title: Brian Gay, four-time PGA Tour winner, is born
14.text: Brian Gay was born in Fort Worth, Texas. Known for his elite putting and short game, he won four PGA Tour titles including a seven-shot rout at the 2009 St. Jude Classic.

14.title: Jane Blalock wins Colgate Triple Crown
14.text: Jane Blalock captured the LPGA Colgate Triple Crown at Mission Hills Country Club in Palm Springs, earning a then-substantial $15,000 winner's check.

14.title: European Tour winner Michael Hoey is born
14.text: Northern Irish golfer Michael Hoey was born December 14, 1979. The 2001 Amateur Championship winner went on to claim five European Tour titles, including the 2011 Madeira Islands Open.

14.title: Davis Love III wins Williams World Challenge
14.text: Davis Love III claimed Tiger Woods's Williams World Challenge at Sherwood Country Club, prevailing in the limited-field season-ending event for the second time in his career.

15.title: PGA Tour player Sam Ryder is born
15.text: American golfer Sam Ryder was born December 15, 1989. He reached the PGA Tour in 2018 and memorably made a hole-in-one on the raucous 16th at the 2022 WM Phoenix Open.

15.title: Julius Boros turns professional
15.text: Julius Boros turned pro and launched a Hall of Fame career, going on to win two U.S. Opens (1952, 1963) and the 1968 PGA Championship, the last at age 48.

15.title: Canadian golf great Stan Leonard dies
15.text: Stan Leonard, a three-time PGA Tour winner and one of Canada's finest players, died at age 90. He claimed eight Canadian PGA titles and contended in major championships into his late 40s.

15.title: Zach Johnson wins Northwestern Mutual World Challenge
15.text: Zach Johnson beat Tiger Woods in a playoff at Sherwood Country Club to win the season-ending World Challenge, denying the host on his home course in a dramatic finish.

15.title: United States retains Presidents Cup at Royal Melbourne
15.text: Trailing entering Sunday singles, the United States rallied at Royal Melbourne to edge Ernie Els's International team 16-14 and retain the Presidents Cup.

16.title: Paul McGinley, 2002 Ryder Cup hero, is born
16.text: Paul McGinley was born in Dublin, Ireland. He holed the winning putt for Europe at the 2002 Ryder Cup and later captained Europe to a commanding victory at Gleneagles in 2014.

16.title: Trevor Immelman, 2008 Masters champion, is born
16.text: Trevor Immelman was born in Cape Town, South Africa. He won the 2008 Masters and later captained the International team at the 2022 Presidents Cup before becoming a TV commentator.

16.title: Lee Westwood wins Cisco World Match Play era title
16.text: Around this time Lee Westwood capped a season of strong off-season golf; his consistency in the early 2000s preceded his rise to world No. 1 in 2010.

16.title: Tiger Woods routs field at Target World Challenge
16.text: Tiger Woods won his Target World Challenge by seven shots at Sherwood, the largest margin in event history, tying the tournament record at 22-under 266 for his fourth title there.

16.title: Arnold Palmer plays in PNC Father-Son Challenge
16.text: Arnold Palmer competed in the PNC Father-Son Challenge near Orlando with his grandson, continuing a beloved holiday tradition in one of the last competitive appearances of the King's storied career.

17.title: Solheim Cup captain Mickey Walker is born
17.text: English golfer Mickey Walker, born this day in 1952, captained Europe in the first four Solheim Cups (1990-1996), winning in 1992. A founding Ladies European Tour member, she was appointed OBE in 1993.

17.title: Major champion Tammie Green is born
17.text: American LPGA golfer Tammie Green, born this day in 1959, was the 1987 Rookie of the Year and won seven LPGA titles, including the 1989 du Maurier Classic major championship.

17.title: Rocco Mediate is born
17.text: American golfer Rocco Mediate, born this day in 1962, famously lost the 2008 U.S. Open to Tiger Woods in a 19-hole playoff at Torrey Pines. He later won the 2016 Senior PGA Championship.

17.title: Players champion Tim Clark is born
17.text: South African Tim Clark, born this day in 1975, won the 2010 Players Championship and was a fixture in the world's top tier through the 2000s, also finishing runner-up at the 2006 Masters.

17.title: Lexi Thompson becomes youngest LET winner
17.text: At 16 years, 10 months, Lexi Thompson won the Dubai Ladies Masters by four strokes to become the youngest professional winner in Ladies European Tour history.

17.title: Cabreras win the PNC Father/Son Challenge
17.text: Angel Cabrera and son Angel Jr. closed with a 12-under 60 for a then-record 25-under 199, beating Team Langer and Team Duval by three strokes in Orlando.

17.title: Langer wins record-tying 5th PNC Championship
17.text: Bernhard and Jason Langer shot a 13-under 59 in heavy wind, opening with seven straight birdies to win at 25-under. It tied Raymond Floyd's record of five PNC titles.

18.title: St Andrews pro Harry Turpie dies
18.text: St Andrews-born professional Harry Turpie, who emigrated to the United States and posted two top-10 U.S. Open finishes in 1900 and 1902, died this day in 1945 at age 70.

18.title: European Tour winner Gavan Levenson is born
18.text: South African Gavan Levenson, born this day in 1953, won two European Tour events and topped the Southern Africa Tour Order of Merit in 1983-84.

18.title: Tour pro turned analyst Charlie Rymer is born
18.text: American Charlie Rymer, born this day in 1967, played the PGA Tour before becoming a popular television analyst for ESPN and Golf Channel.

18.title: Golf immortal Bobby Jones dies at 69
18.text: Bobby Jones, the amateur who completed golf's original Grand Slam in 1930 and co-founded Augusta National and the Masters, died this day in 1971 in Atlanta.

18.title: Irish Open champion Mikko Ilonen is born
18.text: Finland's Mikko Ilonen, born this day in 1979, won the 2000 Amateur Championship and five European Tour titles, including the 2014 Irish Open, as the first Finn to win on the European Tour.

18.title: Two-time Tour winner D.J. Trahan is born
18.text: American D.J. Trahan, born this day in 1980, was the 2000 U.S. Amateur Public Links champion and won twice on the PGA Tour in 2008.

18.title: Singhs win PNC with back-to-back 59s
18.text: Vijay and Qass Singh fired 59-59 to win the PNC Championship at 26-under, one ahead of Team Daly and Team Thomas, the first duo to card two sub-60 rounds in one PNC.

19.title: New Zealand Open great Andrew Shaw is born
19.text: Scottish-born golfer Andrew Shaw, born this day in 1898, built his career in New Zealand, winning seven New Zealand Open and seven New Zealand PGA titles, 14 national championships in all.

19.title: Canadian LPGA star Lorie Kane is born
19.text: Canadian golfer Lorie Kane, born this day in 1964 in Charlottetown, won four LPGA Tour titles and was inducted into both the Canadian Golf Hall of Fame and Canada's Sports Hall of Fame.

19.title: Scotland's Chris Doak is born
19.text: Scottish golfer Chris Doak, born this day in 1977, won the 2012 Open de Lyon on the Challenge Tour and qualified for both the 2013 and 2014 U.S. Opens, making the cut each time.

19.title: LET winner Camille Chevalier is born
19.text: French golfer Camille Chevalier, born this day in 1993, won the 2017 Hero Women's Indian Open and was named the Ladies European Tour Rookie of the Year that season.

19.title: DP World Tour winner Tom McKibbin is born
19.text: Northern Irish golfer Tom McKibbin, born this day in 2002 in Belfast, won the 2023 Porsche European Open and the 2025 Hong Kong Open before joining LIV Golf.

19.title: Team Daly wins PNC with record 27-under
19.text: John Daly and John Daly II shot a final-round 57 to finish 27-under, a tournament record, holding off Tiger and Charlie Woods by two strokes in Orlando.

20.title: Amateur great Johnny Dawson is born
20.text: American amateur golfer and course architect Johnny Dawson, born this day in 1902, was runner-up at the 1947 U.S. Amateur, a winning 1949 Walker Cup player, and won the 1942 Bing Crosby Pro-Am.

20.title: Open runner-up Syd Scott is born
20.text: English golfer Syd Scott, born this day in 1913, finished runner-up at the 1954 Open Championship, played on the 1955 Ryder Cup team, and won the 1964 PGA Seniors Championship.

20.title: U.S. amateur double-winner Jennifer Song is born
20.text: American LPGA golfer Jennifer Song, born this day in 1989, won both the U.S. Women's Amateur and the U.S. Women's Amateur Public Links in 2009, a rare double.

20.title: Major champion Hannah Green is born
20.text: Australian golfer Hannah Green, born this day in 1996 in Perth, won the 2019 Women's PGA Championship for her first major and first LPGA win, and reached World No. 5.

20.title: Filipino pioneer Ben Arda dies
20.text: Filipino golfer Ben Arda, the first Filipino to qualify for the Masters and The Open, died this day in 2006. He won 15 pro titles, including three Philippine Opens and four Japan Golf Tour events.

20.title: Team Thomas wins the PNC Championship
20.text: Justin and Mike Thomas shot a 15-under 57 with no bogeys in their debut to win the PNC Championship by one over Vijay and Qass Singh. Tiger and Charlie Woods finished seventh.

21.title: "Father of pro golf" Walter Hagen is born
21.text: American Walter Hagen, born this day in 1892, won 11 professional majors, third all-time, including four Open Championships and a record-tying five PGA Championships, all in match play.

21.title: Open champion Kel Nagle is born
21.text: Australian Kel Nagle, born this day in 1920, won the 1960 Centenary Open at St Andrews, edging Arnold Palmer by one stroke at age 39 for his lone major championship.

21.title: Irish great Christy O'Connor Snr is born
21.text: Ireland's Christy O'Connor Snr, born this day in 1924, won 64 professional titles and played a record 10 consecutive Ryder Cups from 1955 to 1973. He entered the World Golf Hall of Fame in 2009.

21.title: Hall of Famer Karrie Webb is born
21.text: Australian Karrie Webb, born this day in 1974, won seven LPGA majors and in 2001 became the youngest player to complete the LPGA Career Grand Slam. She entered the Hall of Fame in 2005.

21.title: Ryder Cup winner Thorbjorn Olesen is born
21.text: Danish golfer Thorbjorn Olesen, born this day in 1989, helped Europe win the 2018 Ryder Cup, beating Jordan Spieth in singles, and won the 2018 Italian Open.

21.title: Peter Lonard wins the Australian Open
21.text: Peter Lonard won the Australian Open at Moonah Links in Victoria, finishing one stroke ahead of Chris Downes and Stephen Leaney despite bogeying the final hole.

22.title: First Olympic golf champion Charles Sands is born
22.text: American Charles Sands, born this day in 1865, won the men's individual golf gold at the 1900 Paris Olympics, becoming the first Olympic golf champion.

22.title: Hall of Famer Jan Stephenson is born
22.text: Australian Jan Stephenson, born this day in 1951, won three LPGA majors, the 1981 du Maurier, 1982 LPGA Championship, and 1983 U.S. Women's Open, and entered the World Golf Hall of Fame in 2019.

22.title: Japan Golf Tour star Yuta Ikeda is born
22.text: Japanese golfer Yuta Ikeda, born this day in 1985, has won more than 20 Japan Golf Tour titles, including two Japan Opens, and was the 2016 money-list winner.

22.title: PGA Tour winner Richy Werenski is born
22.text: American Richy Werenski, born this day in 1991, won the 2020 Barracuda Championship for his lone PGA Tour title after playing college golf at Georgia Tech.

22.title: Team Langer beats Team Woods in PNC playoff
22.text: Bernhard Langer made a 15-foot eagle on the first playoff hole to beat Tiger and Charlie Woods after both teams posted 116. It was Langer's sixth PNC title; Charlie Woods made his first career ace.

23.title: Trailblazer Herman Barron is born
23.text: American Herman Barron, born this day in 1909, became the first Jewish golfer to win an official PGA Tour event at the 1942 Western Open. He won four Tour titles and the 1963 Senior PGA.

23.title: Two-time PGA Tour winner Daniel Chopra is born
23.text: Swedish golfer Daniel Chopra, born this day in 1973 in Stockholm, won the 2007 Ginn sur Mer Classic and the 2008 Mercedes-Benz Championship on the PGA Tour.

23.title: LPGA of Japan winner Ayako Uehara is born
23.text: Japanese golfer Ayako Uehara, born this day in 1983 in Okinawa, won three LPGA of Japan Tour titles and in 2016 tied an LPGA record with two holes-in-one in a single tournament at the Canadian Pacific Women's Open.

23.title: Amateur trailblazer Nick Dunlap is born
23.text: American Nick Dunlap, born this day in 2003, won the 2024 American Express as an amateur, the first amateur to win on the PGA Tour since 1991, then won the Barracuda Championship as a pro.

23.title: Japanese legend Jumbo Ozaki dies at 78
23.text: Masashi "Jumbo" Ozaki, the winningest Japanese professional with 94 Japan Golf Tour titles and a 2011 World Golf Hall of Fame inductee, died of cancer this day in 2025 at age 78.

24.title: Amateur legend John Ball is born
24.text: England's John Ball, born this day in 1861, won a record eight Amateur Championships and the 1890 Open. He was the first amateur and first non-Scot to win the Open, matched only by Bobby Jones in winning both in one year.

24.title: Back-to-back U.S. Amateur champion H.J. Whigham is born
24.text: Scottish-born H.J. Whigham, born this day in 1869, won the U.S. Amateur in consecutive years, 1896 and 1897, becoming one of the dominant amateur golfers of the era.

24.title: Olympic golf medalist Frederick Semple is born
24.text: American golfer and tennis player Frederick Semple, born this day in 1872 in St. Louis, was part of the U.S. team that won silver in the team golf event at the 1904 Summer Olympics.

24.title: Australian tour winner Bob Shaw is born
24.text: Australian golfer Bob Shaw, born this day in 1944 in Sydney, won the 1972 Tallahassee Open on the PGA Tour and added several PGA Tour of Australia titles in the early 1980s.

24.title: Winningest golfer ever Kathy Whitworth dies at 83
24.text: LPGA legend Kathy Whitworth died this day in 2022. Her 88 LPGA Tour titles are the most professional wins of any golfer, male or female, and she also won six majors.

25.title: Pioneering golf course architect Ida Dixon is born
25.text: Ida Dixon was born in Philadelphia. She is regarded as the first female golf course architect in the United States, designing the 18-hole course at Springhaven Club in Wallingford, Pennsylvania, in 1904.

25.title: Young Tom Morris dies on Christmas Day at age 24
25.text: Tommy Morris Jr., a four-time Open champion and golf's first prodigy, died at St Andrews on Christmas Day 1875, just months after the deaths of his wife and child. He remains the youngest man to win The Open.

25.title: Golf architect William Flynn is born
25.text: William Flynn, a leading figure of the Philadelphia School of golf design, was born this day. His work includes the redesign of Shinnecock Hills along with Cherry Hills and extensive work at Merion.

25.title: Golf trailblazer Mianne Bagger is born
25.text: Danish-Australian golfer Mianne Bagger was born on Christmas Day. In 2004 she became the first openly transgender woman to play in a professional tournament and to earn Ladies European Tour eligibility.

25.title: European Tour winner Jean-Francois Lucquin is born
25.text: French golfer Jean-Francois Lucquin was born this day. He won the 2008 Omega European Masters, defeating Rory McIlroy with a birdie on the second playoff hole for his first European Tour title.

25.title: PGA Tour player Nicholas Thompson is born
25.text: American golfer Nicholas Thompson, who has competed on the PGA Tour and Korn Ferry Tour, was born on Christmas Day. He is the older brother of LPGA Tour star Lexi Thompson.

26.title: Scottish golfer Tom Dobson is born
26.text: Tom Dobson was born in Gullane, East Lothian. He won the 1925 Scottish Amateur Championship before turning professional later that year and competing in The Open.

26.title: 1899 U.S. Open champion Willie Smith dies
26.text: Scottish golfer Willie Smith died of pneumonia in Mexico City. He won the 1899 U.S. Open by 11 strokes, a record margin that stood until Tiger Woods won by 15 in 2000.

26.title: Amateur standout Jim Holtgrieve is born
26.text: American amateur Jim Holtgrieve was born in St. Louis. He won the inaugural U.S. Mid-Amateur in 1981 and played on three winning Walker Cup teams before later captaining the U.S. side.

26.title: Senior major winner Ken Tanigawa is born
26.text: American golfer Ken Tanigawa was born this day. He captured the 2019 Senior PGA Championship at Oak Hill, a senior major, after being named PGA Tour Champions Rookie of the Year in 2018.

26.title: 1928 Amateur champion Phil Perkins dies
26.text: English golfer Phil Perkins died this day. He won the 1928 Amateur Championship, was runner-up to Bobby Jones in the 1928 U.S. Amateur, and tied for second at the 1932 U.S. Open.

26.title: First Chilean LPGA winner Nicole Perrot is born
26.text: Chilean golfer Nicole Perrot was born this day. She became the first Chilean-born player to win on the LPGA Tour, capturing the 2005 Longs Drugs Challenge.

27.title: Dave Marr, 1965 PGA Champion, is born
27.text: American golfer Dave Marr, who won the 1965 PGA Championship at Laurel Valley by two strokes over Jack Nicklaus and Billy Casper and later became a respected TV commentator, was born in Houston, Texas.

27.title: Steve Jones, 1996 U.S. Open champion, is born
27.text: American golfer Steve Jones, who captured the 1996 U.S. Open at Oakland Hills by one stroke over Tom Lehman and Davis Love III, was born in Artesia, New Mexico.

27.title: Australian winner Terry Price is born
27.text: Australian golfer Terry Price, a multiple winner on the PGA Tour of Australasia and the 2004 New Zealand Open champion, was born in Rockhampton, Queensland.

27.title: Two-time major winner Sherri Steinhauer is born
27.text: American golfer Sherri Steinhauer, an eight-time LPGA Tour winner whose titles included two majors, the 1992 du Maurier Classic and the 2006 Women's British Open, was born in Madison, Wisconsin.

27.title: Sophie Gustafson is born
27.text: Swedish golfer Sophie Gustafson, a five-time LPGA Tour winner who claimed the 2000 Women's British Open and played on eight European Solheim Cup teams, was born in Varberg, Sweden.

27.title: Four-time PGA Tour winner Charley Hoffman is born
27.text: American golfer Charley Hoffman, a four-time PGA Tour winner whose victories include the 2010 Deutsche Bank Championship and 2016 Valero Texas Open, was born in San Diego, California.

28.title: Johnny Goodman, last amateur U.S. Open champ, is born
28.text: American amateur Johnny Goodman, who in 1933 became the last amateur to win the U.S. Open and added the 1937 U.S. Amateur title, was born in Omaha, Nebraska.

28.title: Hubert Green, two-time major champion, is born
28.text: American golfer Hubert Green, a 19-time PGA Tour winner who claimed the 1977 U.S. Open and 1985 PGA Championship and reached the World Golf Hall of Fame, was born in Birmingham, Alabama.

28.title: PGA Tour winner and broadcaster Curt Byrum is born
28.text: American golfer and broadcaster Curt Byrum, whose lone PGA Tour victory came at the 1989 Hardee's Golf Classic, was born in Onida, South Dakota.

28.title: Asian Tour record-winner Thaworn Wiratchant is born
28.text: Thai golfer Thaworn Wiratchant, who became the all-time record holder for most Asian Tour victories and twice topped the tour's money list, was born in Bangkok, Thailand.

28.title: Three-time Masters champion Jimmy Demaret dies
28.text: American golfer Jimmy Demaret, the first three-time Masters winner (1940, 1947, 1950) and a 31-time PGA Tour champion known for his colorful attire, died of a heart attack in Houston at age 73.

28.title: Martin Kaymer, two-time major champion, is born
28.text: German golfer Martin Kaymer, who won the 2010 PGA Championship and the 2014 U.S. Open and reached world number one in 2011, was born in Dusseldorf, Germany.

28.title: Malaysian Olympian Gavin Green is born
28.text: Malaysian golfer Gavin Green, a multiple Olympian and standout collegiate player who turned professional after a decorated career at New Mexico, was born in Malaysia.

29.title: 1930s PGA Tour star Jimmy Hines is born
29.text: American golfer Jimmy Hines was born December 29, 1903. He won nine PGA Tour events in the 1930s and was named to the 1939 U.S. Ryder Cup team, though that match was cancelled by World War II.

29.title: Legendary course architect Pete Dye is born
29.text: Golf course architect Pete Dye was born December 29, 1925. He designed iconic layouts including TPC Sawgrass and Whistling Straits and entered the World Golf Hall of Fame in 2008.

29.title: Filipino golf great Frankie Minoza is born
29.text: Filipino golfer Frankie Minoza was born December 29, 1959. He won seven Japan Golf Tour titles and twice topped the Asia Golf Circuit Order of Merit during a long international career.

29.title: Singaporean trailblazer Lam Chih Bing is born
29.text: Singaporean golfer Lam Chih Bing was born December 29, 1976. He won the 2008 Volvo Masters of Asia and became the first Singaporean to make the cut at a major championship.

29.title: Four-time PGA Tour winner Martin Laird is born
29.text: Scottish golfer Martin Laird was born December 29, 1982, in Glasgow. He won four PGA Tour titles, including the 2011 Arnold Palmer Invitational and the 2013 Valero Texas Open.

30.title: Australian golf pioneer Patricia Bridges is born
30.text: Australian golf administrator and player Patricia Bridges was born December 30, 1921. The Women's Australian Open trophy is named in her honour, and she became Golf Australia's only life member.

30.title: Architect Devereux Emmet dies
30.text: Pioneering American golf course architect Devereux Emmet died December 30, 1934. He designed more than 150 courses, including early layouts at Bethpage and Congressional.

30.title: Argentine champion Florentino Molina is born
30.text: Argentine golfer Florentino Molina was born December 30, 1938. He won the Argentine Open a remarkable five times during his career.

30.title: LPGA winner Kris Tschetter is born
30.text: American LPGA golfer Kris Tschetter was born December 30, 1964. She won the 1992 Northgate Computer Classic for her sole LPGA Tour title.

30.title: Seven-time LPGA winner Michelle McGann is born
30.text: American golfer Michelle McGann was born December 30, 1969. Known for her wide-brimmed hats, she won seven LPGA Tour events during her career.

30.title: Tiger Woods is born in Cypress, California
30.text: Eldrick 'Tiger' Woods was born December 30, 1975. He went on to win 15 major championships and tie the all-time record of 82 PGA Tour victories, becoming one of the greatest golfers in history.

31.title: PGA Tour winner Randy Glover is born
31.text: American golfer Randy Glover was born December 31, 1941. He claimed his lone PGA Tour title at the 1967 Azalea Open, winning a playoff over Joe Campbell.

31.title: Six-time PGA Tour winner Bob Gilder is born
31.text: American golfer Bob Gilder was born December 31, 1950. He won six PGA Tour events and is famed for his albatross at the 18th that helped him win the 1982 Westchester Classic.

31.title: PGA Tour winner Adam Svensson is born
31.text: Canadian golfer Adam Svensson was born December 31, 1993. He won his first PGA Tour title at the 2022 RSM Classic, closing with a six-under 64.

31.title: Two-time PGA Tour winner J.L. Lewis dies
31.text: American golfer J.L. Lewis died December 31, 2019, at age 59 after a battle with multiple myeloma. He won the 1999 John Deere Classic and the 2003 84 Lumber Classic on the PGA Tour.

31.title: Ryder Cup player Barry Lane dies
31.text: English golfer Barry Lane died December 31, 2022. He won five European Tour events and played for Europe in the 1993 Ryder Cup.
